I drift through the vacuum, hoping this celestial flare pushes me toward a viable host.

I seek to overwrite the existing genetic sequence and convert this foreign space into my own factory.
I drift toward the shimmering rift, seeking a fresh host to hijack and replicate within.

I must find a living organism before my protein shell degrades in the sunlight.

I drift in the void of this sterile medium with no cell membrane to breach.

I remain encased in my protein shell, waiting for a chance encounter that never comes.
My genetic sequence stays locked and dormant while I float through this endless, empty expanse.
There are no receptors to bind with in this stillness, leaving my payload unreleased and useless.

I sense the surge of interferon signaling the nearby cells to lock their gates against me.

My surface proteins are failing to latch as the antibodies swarm to neutralize my entry point.

I guard this cytoplasm with every protein spike to ensure my genome remains undisturbed.
